ul.li: File descriptor table — dup2() only affects child
Job table — child has empty table
Open file offsets — both point to same kernel file structure
If child writes to fd 3, parent sees offset advance
This is why (cmd >file) works but (cmd >file) ; cat file might race

div.p: Key Insight: c // In child after fork: chdir("/tmp"); // Only child's cwd changes setenv("X", "1"); // Only child's environ changes x = "inner"; // Only child's vars change // But: write(fd3, ...); // Offset shared with parent! _exit(status); // Only exit status returns

ul.li: Data pages: On first write only
Child has copy of parent's buffers
